title: --read-write-tmpfs=false should set /dev/* tmpfs to readonly
body:
Change the --read-only-tmpfs option to --read-write-tmpfs since
this makes sense and the old name was doing the exact opposite.

Fixes: #12937

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
`/dev` is still used by the OCI runtime after setting the mounts.

I've fixed an issue in crun so it works when `--tty` is not used, but for a read-only `/dev` also in the tty case, we'd need to set the `ro` flag later.  I'll take a look at it.

I've tried with runc and it fails as well, so I think it should be optional to set `/dev` read-only.  Could we extend `--read-only-tmpfs` to accept a string and have something like `--read-only-tmpfs=all`?
this seems to help with your PR: https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/859
@kolyshkin  WDYT?
I think if we leave any tmpfs writable, we don't solve the problem.
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12937

As @giuseppe mentioned earlier, runc can't work with ro /dev, but this fix is easy https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/pull/3345 (and very similar to https://github.com/containers/crun/pull/857).

One other thing, @rhatdan, I also find it very confusing how `--read-only-tmpfs=false` results in read-only `/dev` mounts. Maybe a separate option (`--read-only-dev`) or something else entirely?

@kolyshkin I am not crazy about adding another function, Since I believe the original goal of --read-only-tmpfs (A badly named option, which we should probably change) was to make the entire container read-only. (At least the way I think about it).
Changed the option to read-write-tmpfs.

This PR is waiting for an updated released version of runc, which will allow it to work with /dev set to read/only mode.
@kolyshkin @AkihiroSuda Any idea when that will be?

> This PR is waiting for an updated released version of runc, which will allow it to work with /dev set to read/only mode.
> @kolyshkin @AkihiroSuda Any idea when that will be?

Depending on how soon you need it.

We can certainly release runc 1.1.1, either now (= soon) with this change alone, or a bit later, waiting for a few more fixes to pile up.

> This PR is waiting for an updated released version of runc,

Backport PR: https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/pull/3355
Well since this is a post 4.0 feature, it is not needed immediately, but soon.

It doesn't look like runc 1.1.1 is release yet

This week (fingers crossed), hopefully today or tomorrow. Preparing the changelog now. See https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/3437 to track progress.

runc 1.1.1 is now released (it is amazing what can be done while being on vacation; perhaps we should all be on vacation permanently). https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/releases/tag/v1.1.1
@haircommander Does this mean you have something to do now, or is this in @cevich box?
lol, probably in my box now :smile:

Q: Can this wait for my F36 PR or should I do a separate image build + update
(old images are building easily/cleanly, my only motivation to fewer PRs is it's one less thing to keep track of).
Update: Nevermind, we need new images anyway for https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/1176

Images built fine, but I just checked and F35 only has runc-1.1.0-1.fc35.x86_64, and we're using the `updates-testing` repo.  :disappointed:   Can somebody press the magic buttons to get this packaged for F35 & 36?
I just noticed v1.1.1 is now in updates-testing, so I'm re-spinning VM images in https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/119 that should pick it up.
...Alright, give `c5706681618268160` a try.
***Note***: This is slightly newer, and contains a fix for multi-arch image building (don't clobber #13739): `c6464310661611520`
Clarification: I'd like to avoid the situation where #13739 merges, then this merges (with the old image ID), wrecking one of the multi-arch build task fixes (the fix is in the VM images).
it seem on ubuntu there is a too old crun version.  The error was fixed in crun-1.4.2
@rhatdan is this still on track for 4.1, it is looking difficult now. Removing the 4.1 label from it, but please add it back if you think it will make it in time.
@edsantiago Please give me a clue as to what is happening here on upgrade tests?

I have no idea. The only instance in which I see pod + freeing + lock is #8279, but that was a flake and got closed a year and a half ago.  This one is consistent.

For the record, the error here is:
```
# # podman pod rm mypod
# time="2022-05-13T05:00:08-05:00" level=error msg="Freeing pod dea923c9c17bc955a4552295d4cd2f58cee71c21e3442b2bbf066d7972ded896 lock: no such file or directory"
# Error: error freeing lock for container ca3fd43a4a31e6a6b9746cde748023c0e967fea53961e82e095a2c81511521e8: no such file or directory
```
My guess is that, with $TMPDIR being ro, something in the running podman processes is croaking. Those logs are not easy to find, so I'll have to take a look on Monday.
Friendly ping. @rhatdan, are you on it?
I look at it once in a while and restart it hoping for a miracle.  But I have no idea why this does not work on Ubuntu.

Hrmmm, you're using recent VM images too :confused:  I see this, which is probably telling:

`runc: runc create failed: unable to start container process: unable to setup user: fchown fd 0: read-only file system: OCI runtime error`

Have you tried running any of those tests or failing commands manually under `hack/get_ci_vm.sh`?  Might help get to the bottom of why/how the filesystems are going/set read-only.
Nope, Have thought about it, but never have time.  Others can take if over and drive to conclusion...

> runc: runc create failed: unable to start container process: unable to setup user: fchown fd 0: read-only file system: OCI runtime error

This was fixed in https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/pull/3345 which was backported to runc 1.1 in https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/pull/3355, and the fix made its way to runc 1.1.1.

What runc version is used in the test?
@cevich would know for sure, but I think it is using the current released versions of runc in Ubuntu.

One of the failing Ubuntu 22.04 tasks reports:

```
Ubuntu 22.04 LTS \n \l
Kernel:  5.15.0-1013-gcp
Cgroups:  tmpfs
dpkg-query: warning: parsing file '/var/lib/dpkg/status' near line 1430 package 'containers-common':
 missing 'Maintainer' field

dpkg-query: no packages found matching cri-o-runc
conmon-2:2.1.2-0ubuntu22.04+obs5.1-amd64
```

Checking if there's an update available...There are 28 package updates available, none of them are runc.  We have an option to bring in a custom-built version, but generally the distro-provided packages would be preferred.  Is there a way we can tell if `runc-1.1.0-0ubuntu1-amd64` is the package with the needed backport?

cc: @lsm5 FYI for now.
This is my fault, #16240. Can you run `make docs`, then `git add` the changed file and `git commit --amend`?
